setting.note: Twain renamed his boyhood Hannibal “St. Petersburg”, but built it exactly: the Mississippi bank, the cave, the graveyard, the whitewashed fence. The town has marked those spots for tourists ever since, which makes it a rare novel a reader can already walk. It brings Mark Twain into the atlas.
